title: DSDS Foundation Definitions
description: Defines documents for foundations: the broad, cross-cutting design domains a system is built on — color, typography, spacing, elevation, motion, shape, accessibility, content, and the like. A foundation document holds the identity and metadata of a domain. Typed entries in the `metadata` array carry modular metadata (status, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, links, and more). Typed entries in the `documentBlocks` array carry structured docs.

foundation.description: The principles, guidelines, scales, and token references that govern a broad design domain across the system — color, typography, spacing, elevation, motion, accessibility, content, and the like. Foundations are wider than a single visual attribute: a domain may be visual (color, spacing) or cross-cutting (accessibility, content). The top level holds identity: `identifier`, `name`, and `description`. The `metadata` array holds modular entries with a `kind` tag (status, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, links, and more). The `documentBlocks` array holds structured docs as typed entries: principle, scale, guideline, accessibility, content, and so on.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a foundation.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier for the foundation (e.g., 'color', 'typography', 'spacing', 'elevation', 'motion', 'shape', 'accessibility', 'content'). MUST be unique within the documentation group.

metadata.description: Modular metadata entries. Each entry is a typed object with a `kind` tag. Kinds include status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this foundation, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Accepts the foundation-specific kinds (principles, scale, motion) plus the general kinds (guideline, use-cases, accessibility, content, examples). The `kind` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters: tools SHOULD preserve it for display. Express internal artifact links (such as token group references) through a `links` metadata entry, not as document block entries.

agents.description: AI-ready context for this foundation: intent, constraints, disambiguation, anti-patterns, examples, and keywords for efficient AI use.
